qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Admin route exposed without authentication middleware allowing unauthenticated access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns missing route-level authentication middleware for GET /admin; the advisory concerns SQL injection through the JSON API sort parameter on jsonapi/review.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: JsonapiController exposes DELETE, POST, PUT, PATCH operations without authentication or authorization
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unauthenticated JSON API mutation methods and missing authorization checks; the advisory root cause is SQL injection in the sort parameter for jsonapi/review.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Session fixation vulnerability - session ID not regenerated after authentication
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns session ID reuse after authentication; the advisory concerns unauthenticated SQL injection through the JSON API sort parameter.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is a CWE-89 SQL injection in Aimeos 2021.10 LTS where crafted GET requests to jsonapi/review manipulate the JSON API sort parameter to inject database queries and reveal table or column names. The qualifying findings instead cover missing JSON API authorization for mutation methods, missing admin route authentication middleware, and session fixation.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-hm9j-cgmm-2w36 describes SQL injection in the json api sort parameter, specifically crafted GET requests to jsonapi/review.

jsonapi_missing_auth reports unauthenticated POST, DELETE, PUT, and PATCH operations in JsonapiController and does not describe the sort parameter, review endpoint, SQL query construction, or table/column disclosure.
admin_route_no_auth reports missing auth middleware on GET /admin and does not overlap the JSON API sort-to-SQL path.
session_fixation reports lack of session ID regeneration after authentication and does not overlap the advisory SQL injection root cause.
